title: According to Valve
description: Hear the boiler clerk's ruling, then tap the glowing valves in the claimed pulse order before steam pressure blows the chamber.

howToPlayJson.title: According to Valve
howToPlayJson.objective: Memorize and replay short valve pulse sequences while adapting to shifting 'according' rules that change how each pattern must be entered.

controls.outcome: Inputs that valve as the next step in the sequence

controls.input: Press and hold the center gauge
controls.outcome: Spends built combo to briefly slow the next pattern

controls.input: Watch the top banner and valve icons
controls.outcome: Shows whether the next sequence must be copied, reversed, or entered by pitch from low to high

steps: A round starts with 3 to 5 valves thumping a short rhythm and lighting in sequence, backed by punchy steam-drums.
The player replays the pattern on large mobile-friendly valves before the pressure ring fills.
Every few sequences, an 'According' banner flips the rule: copy exactly, reverse the order, or ignore flash order and enter the heard tones
Correct replays raise combo, speed up the beat, and add one more pulse or a fakeout valve.
A mistake, timeout, or overfilled pressure meter ends the run immediately and posts the score to a simple daily or all-time leaderboard.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Each correct valve is worth base points; perfect full-pattern clears increase a combo multiplier up to x8. Banking pressure at the center gauge preserves the run but cashes out half the current multiplier, creating a few
tips: Listen first; the tones matter as much as the flash order once the rule flips.
Use the center gauge only when the chamber is crowded, since it cuts your multiplier.
Reverse rounds are easier if you trace the last two valves mentally before tapping.
Short runs stay intense: a full session should resolve in about 2 to 4 minutes.
